pi_items.metadata_value: Oral history interview with John Lewis
pi_items.verified_value: Oral History Interview with John Lewis
pi_items.source: Multiple oral histories exist: (1) Southern Oral History Program Collection (SOHP) Interview A-0073 from November 20, 1973, conducted by Jack Bass and Walter DeVries; (2) Explorations in Black Leadership project at UVA where Julian Bond interviewed John Lewis

pi_items.verified_value: Julian Bond (interviewer) and John Lewis (interviewee) conducted interview September 26, 2005, Congressional Recording Studio, Washington, D.C., as part of Explorations in Black Leadership project (https://blackleadership.virginia.edu/interview/lewis-john/). However, the 1973 SOHP interview was conducted by Jack Bass and Walter DeVries.
pi_items.source: Explorations in Black Leadership webpage (blackleadership.virginia.edu) and Southern Oral History Program Collection (docsouth.unc.edu)

pi_items.verified_value: Two real interviews found: (1) November 20, 1973 (SOHP, conducted by Bass and DeVries); (2) September 26, 2005 (Explorations in Black Leadership, conducted by Julian Bond). No verified 2013 interview found.
pi_items.source: SOHP A-0073 (docsouth.unc.edu) and Explorations in Black Leadership (blackleadership.virginia.edu); Civil Rights History Project collection does not list John Lewis among 52 interviews conducted in 2013

pi_items.verified_value: No Library of Congress item with ID 2015026020 exists (HTTP 404 error when accessed). The Julian Bond-John Lewis interview is hosted at https://blackleadership.virginia.edu (University of Virginia), not loc.gov.
pi_items.source: Direct HTTP HEAD request to https://www.loc.gov/item/2015026020/ returned HTTP/2 404. The Bond-Lewis interview is documented at blackleadership.virginia.edu

pi_items.verified_value: Both identified interviews (1973 SOHP and 2005 UVA) are available as transcripts and audio/video. Specifically, the UVA Explorations in Black Leadership interview includes both transcript and video recording.
pi_items.source: Explorations in Black Leadership project provides transcript and video; SOHP A-0073 provides transcript and audio

privileged_info_summary: There are real oral history interviews with John Lewis conducted by prominent civil rights scholars. John Lewis was interviewed as part of the Southern Oral History Program (November 20, 1973) by Jack Bass and Walter DeVries, where he discussed his role as SNCC chairman. Additionally, Julian Bond interviewed John Lewis on September 26, 2005, at the Congressional Recording Studio in Washington, D.C., as part of the Explorations in Black Leadership project at the University of Virginia. Both interviews are available as transcripts. However, no verified interview exists from November 20, 2013, and the Library of Congress URL cited in the task (loc.gov/item/2015026020/) does not exist (returns 404 error).
